projects.detail.title: Working with a project
projects.detail.summary: Use the project page to review project information, follow activity, manage progress items, and maintain participants.
projects.detail.whenToUse: Open this help from the project detail page.
steps: Review the project details card to confirm the owner, status, and main description.
Use the activity section to inspect recent work with the available date filters.
Manage achievements, goals, and highlights from their respective cards on the page.
Use the team and member sections to add or remove participants when your permissions allow it.
rules: Some actions only appear for users with the required project permissions.
Destructive actions such as delete require confirmation before the change is sent.

projects.members.title: Managing project members
projects.members.summary: Project members are managed directly from the project page through add, role update, and remove actions.
projects.members.whenToUse: Use this help in the members card on a project page.
steps: Open the project page and locate the members section.
Add a member from the available employee list when the add action is visible.
Complete any required fields such as role or collaboration description before saving.
Use the existing member actions to update their role or remove them from the project.
rules: Member management depends on project permissions such as PROJ-05-02 and PROJ-06-02.
Changes in this section should update the current project context, not a global employee profile.

projects.teams.title: Managing project teams
projects.teams.summary: Teams can be attached to a project from the project page so their work is tracked inside the same project context.
projects.teams.whenToUse: Use this help in the teams card on a project page.
steps: Open the teams section in the project page.
Select an available team to connect it to the project.
Complete any required assignment field such as the team role before saving.
Remove a team from the project from the same section if it should no longer be associated.
rules: Team assignment belongs to the project-team relationship, not to the team record itself.
Removing a team from a project only affects that project relationship.

reports.view.title: Viewing a generated report
reports.view.summary: The report page shows the generated sections for the selected employee, team, or project and lets you copy the output.
reports.view.whenToUse: Open this help from the report view page.
steps: Use the report sections to review the generated summary in order.
Use the copy button to copy the full report content.
Use the back button to return to the page where the report was generated.
rules: A report view depends on an existing generated report.
The page displays the saved output instead of regenerating the report.

reports.generate.title: Generating a report
reports.generate.summary: The report generator creates a report for an employee, team, or project using a selected subject and date range.
reports.generate.whenToUse: Use this help from the home page generator or the compact generator shown on detail pages.
steps: Select the report content type when the full generator is shown.
Choose the subject or keep the current page subject in compact mode.
Pick a quick range or open the calendar to set a custom period.
Submit the form and open the generated result from the confirmation area.
rules: The start date must be before the end date.
The generator only succeeds when the selected subject has activity in the requested range.

admin.roles.title: Managing roles and privileges
admin.roles.summary: The roles administration page lets you create roles, remove roles, and toggle privileges in the matrix.
admin.roles.whenToUse: Open this help from the roles administration page.
steps: Review the privilege matrix to see which role currently has each permission.
Use the toggle in a matrix cell to enable or disable a privilege for a role.
Create a new role from the create role action when a new access profile is needed.
Remove a role from its delete action after confirming it is no longer needed.
rules: The page depends on admin permissions such as ADMIN-01, ADMIN-02, ADMIN-03, and ADMIN-04.
Privilege changes affect which actions are visible in other modules.

shared.action_popup.title: Using popup forms
shared.action_popup.summary: Many actions in the system open a popup to confirm a change or complete a short form.
shared.action_popup.whenToUse: Used across project, team, and administration screens.
steps: Open the popup from the action button.
Review the title and the short description before continuing.
Complete the required fields and submit the form.
Use cancel to close the popup without saving.
rules: Delete and remove actions should always be reviewed before submission.
Popup forms reuse the same validation and flash message patterns as other forms.

shared.forms.title: Completing forms
shared.forms.summary: Forms in this app follow the same pattern for required fields, validation, submission, and feedback.
shared.forms.whenToUse: Used by create, edit, and popup flows across the system.
steps: Complete the required fields shown in the current form.
Review field formats such as dates, text length, and selected relationships.
Submit the form and wait for the page or section to refresh.
Review the success or error message after the request finishes.
rules: Required fields must be completed before the request can succeed.
Server-side validation still applies even when the form uses AJAX.

shared.membership_actions.title: Membership actions
shared.membership_actions.summary: Membership actions are used to connect employees or teams to a project or team and to remove those relationships later.
shared.membership_actions.whenToUse: Used in project members, project teams, and team participants sections.
steps: Open the section where the relationship is managed.
Choose the person or team you want to add.
Complete any required contextual field such as role, description, or assignment.
Save the relationship and verify it appears in the current section.
rules: Membership data belongs to the relationship record, not only to the employee or team record.
Removing a relationship should only affect the current module context.

shared.role_assignment.title: Role assignment
shared.role_assignment.summary: Role fields define the responsibility of a member inside a specific project, team, or administration context.
shared.role_assignment.whenToUse: Used in project member management, team member management, and admin role assignment.
steps: Locate the current role control in the section you are editing.
Select the new role or submit the role field in the add form.
Confirm the role change in the current page after the request succeeds.
rules: A role change in one context does not automatically change unrelated contexts.
Only users with the matching permission should be able to change role assignments.

shared.permissions.title: Permissions and hidden actions
shared.permissions.summary: The application hides or blocks actions when the current account does not have the required permission.
shared.permissions.whenToUse: Relevant on all administration and management pages.
steps: If an expected action is missing, verify the current account role and privilege assignment.
Check the related administration page if the action depends on a role privilege.
Refresh the page after a role or privilege change to confirm the updated access.
rules: Permissions control both page access and specific actions inside a page.
An action may be visible only to the project responsible person or to a role with the right privilege.

shared.flash_messages.title: Status and flash messages
shared.flash_messages.summary: Success, warning, and error messages confirm whether a form or AJAX action completed correctly.
shared.flash_messages.whenToUse: Used after create, update, delete, toggle, and report generation actions.
steps: Submit the action from the current page or popup.
Read the returned flash message to confirm the result.
Retry the action only after reviewing the message when an error appears.
rules: A success message confirms the backend accepted the change.
An error message means the page or section should be reviewed before repeating the request.

shared.report_date_range.title: Choosing a report date range
shared.report_date_range.summary: The report generator supports quick presets and manual calendar selection for the reporting period.
shared.report_date_range.whenToUse: Use this help inside the report generator.
steps: Start with a quick range if one matches the reporting period you need.
Open the calendar when you need a custom start and end date.
Apply the range and verify both dates before generating the report.
rules: The available preset behavior may change depending on the report type.
A range with no activity will not generate a report.
